name: Present Tense â€” Key Irregular Verbs
nameEs: El Presente â€” Verbos Irregulares Clave
summary: Master the most important irregular verbs: ir, tener, hacer, poder, and querer â€” you'll use them in every conversation.
explanation:
The most frequently used verbs in Spanish are irregular â€” they don't follow the standard -ar/-er/-ir patterns. The good news is there are only about 20 truly irregular verbs you need at the beginner level, and many follow predictable patterns.

Stem-changing verbs are the most common type of irregularity. The stem vowel changes in all forms except nosotros and vosotros: querer (eâ†’ie), poder (oâ†’ue), jugar (uâ†’ue). These are sometimes called 'boot verbs' because if you draw a line around the forms that change, it looks like a boot.

Some verbs are only irregular in the 'yo' form: hacerâ†’hago, ponerâ†’pongo, salirâ†’salgo, conocerâ†’conozco. These are called 'yo-go' verbs. A few verbs like ir (to go) and ser (to be) are completely irregular and must be memorised individually.
pattern: Stem-changing (eâ†’ie, oâ†’ue, eâ†’i) in all forms except nosotros/vosotros | 'Yo-go' verbs: irregular only in yo form

examples.en: I go to work by metro (ir)

commonMistakes: Regularising ir: 'yo io' should be 'yo voy'
Forgetting the stem change: 'yo quero' should be 'yo quiero' (eâ†’ie)
Applying the stem change to nosotros: 'nosotros podemos' is correct (no change), not 'puodemos'
Mixing up tener and haber: 'tengo 25 aĂ±os' (I'm 25) is correct â€” age uses tener
Forgetting the -go in yo forms: 'yo haco' should be 'yo hago'
tip: Focus on the five verbs in this lesson â€” ir, tener, hacer, poder, querer. They appear in almost every conversation and cover going places, having things, doing activities, ability, and desire.
